recent_mandate_wins: New York State Common Retirement Fund
known_strengths: Broad product shelf across public fixed income
known_weaknesses: Fees can be higher than passive-oriented competitors
Large-platform complexity can make customization slower
May be viewed as crowded in flagship strategies
notes: Main competitive threat in the CalPERS fixed income landscape. Strong ESG credibility and consultant familiarity make PIMCO especially difficult to displace in large mandates.

recent_mandate_losses: California State Teachers' Retirement System - active credit search
known_strengths: Massive scale and broad fixed income platform
known_weaknesses: Can be perceived as less differentiated for alpha-seeking mandates
Large asset base may raise concerns about agility in niche sectors
notes: A strong finalist threat for CalPERS if fee competitiveness and reporting quality are weighted heavily.

recent_mandate_wins: Los Angeles County Employees Retirement Association
known_strengths: Long track record in active fixed income
known_weaknesses: ESG narrative is less compelling than top-tier competitors
Performance can be cyclical depending on credit environment
notes: Respected incumbent-style competitor with credibility in traditional fixed income, though less formidable than PIMCO on ESG and broader platform breadth.

recent_mandate_wins: Virginia Retirement System passive bond sleeve
recent_mandate_losses: State of Wisconsin Investment Board active bond search
known_strengths: Trusted institution for large plan indexing needs
known_weaknesses: Less compelling for active alpha-oriented searches
Limited differentiation in bespoke public pension reporting
notes: Biggest risk comes when CalPERS frames the decision against low-cost passive implementation rather than active value-add.

recent_mandate_wins: Massachusetts Pension Reserves Investment Management Board
known_strengths: Solid credit and securitized research resources
Good balance of pricing and active management capability
known_weaknesses: Less differentiated consultant mindshare in large public pension finals
ESG positioning not usually viewed as a primary selling point
notes: Competitive on quality and price, but generally not the first name that dominates a CalPERS-style headline search.

recent_mandate_wins: Maryland State Retirement and Pension System
known_strengths: Credible global and emerging market debt capabilities
Stable investment teams in many strategies
known_weaknesses: Can be less fee-competitive in large institutional searches
Less dominant than mega-firms in consultant-led finals
notes: More threatening in global credit or EMD searches than in plain-vanilla core fixed income.

recent_mandate_wins: Pennsylvania Public School Employees' Retirement System
recent_mandate_losses: Texas County and District Retirement System
known_strengths: Strong structured products and global macro insights
known_weaknesses: Brand can trigger questions around fee levels
Some public funds view platform as more opportunistic than benchmark-aware
notes: A credible incumbent-caliber competitor at CalPERS, especially where complex credit or macro positioning are valued.

recent_mandate_losses: California Public Employees' Retirement System shortlist - prior cycle
known_strengths: Strong manager bench and team continuity
Good ESG resources and reporting quality
Competitive across both benchmarked and flexible mandates
known_weaknesses: May be viewed as less nimble than specialist boutiques
Pricing not always best-in-class versus passive-heavy firms
notes: A serious finalist risk for CalPERS due to balanced strength across performance, team depth, ESG, and reporting.
